opps.title: Section 508 Compliance Support Services
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
****This requirement will be a single award IDV so APFS is not required.  This is marked as no longer planned and republished for vendor awareness.****

CISA National Risk Management Center (NRMC) seeks Section 508 compliance support services to assist the organization in ensuring information and communications technology (ICT), electronic content, digital communications, and associated documentation comply with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. Â§ 794d), and applicable accessibility standards.

Services will include accessibility evaluations, remediation, testing support, documentation review, compliance reporting, consultation, and guidance related to accessibility best practices and federal accessibility requirements. The requirement will support the organization in identifying accessibility deficiencies, reducing compliance risk, and improving accessibility for individuals with disabilities through remediation actions.

Technical support for electronic documents, websites, web applications, software platforms, multimedia products, training materials, and other digital content produced or maintained by the organization will be required.   Section 508 conformance testing of web content and web applications using the DHS Trusted Tester Section 508 Conformance Test Process for Web and evaluation and remediation of electronic documents (e.g., Microsoft Office and PDF) to meet Section 508 requirements using federally recognized accessibility testing methods for documents will be required.

opps.title: CISA Stakeholder Engagement and Analytics Support Services
opps.description: The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) requires strategic relations, stakeholder engagement, analytics, and program support services to advance CISAâ€™s mission through coordinated outreach, partnership development, awareness initiatives, stakeholder data management, and performance evaluation. SED serves as CISAâ€™s focal point for enterprise stakeholder engagement, supporting relationships with federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, critical infrastructure partners, private industry, academia, non-profit organizations, and the public. Support includes planning and executing stakeholder engagement strategies; managing awareness and outreach campaigns; supporting Systemically Important Entities (SIE) activities; facilitating Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA) implementation efforts; supporting cybersecurity workforce and academic partnership initiatives; and maintaining stakeholder collaboration and information-sharing activities. The contractor shall provide expertise in stakeholder analysis, program evaluation, data governance, performance measurements, communications, and project management, including support for CISAâ€™s Stakeholder Relationship Management (SRM) platform, analytics, reporting and visualization capabilities. Support shall include collection, integration, analysis, and reporting of stakeholder and program data to assess outcomes, improve decision-making, identity trend, and enhance mission effectiveness. Work will be performed at a combination of contractor facilities and the Department of Homeland Security facilities as required.

opps.title: INTEGRATED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES
opps.description:
The Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC) seeks contractor support to enhance recruiting operations through program management, analytical services, and advanced information technology. A key focus of this requirement is the development, deployment, and sustainment of custom business applications using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI). The contractor will deliver business process automation solutions, including Robotic Process Automation (RPA), to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and support data-driven decision-making.

Additionally, the contractor will provide graphic design and content creation services to support both internal and external communications. This includes designing user interfaces for applications, developing marketing materials, and producing visually engaging content for outreach and stakeholder engagement. All content must align with Coast Guard branding and approval protocols.

Other services include planning and analysis led by Project Management Professional (PMP) certified personnel, performance management, strategic planning, meeting facilitation, documentation, process improvement, change coordination, reporting, and knowledge management. Personnel must have relevant certifications and government experience.

The period of performance is one base year plus four option years. Work will primarily occur at Coast Guard Headquarters in Washington DC, with possible off-site work as approved. All contractor staff must meet pre-employment screening and background investigation requirements.

opps.title: Application Development and Support Services - FMCS
opps.description:
The General Services Administration, Office of Centralized Acquisition Services, on behalf of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service, is issuing this Request for Quote to establish a single-award Blanket Purchase Agreement under GSA Multiple Award Schedule SIN 54151S Information Technology Professional Services.

FMCS requires Application Development and Support Services to maintain, support, modernize, secure, integrate, and enhance its enterprise application environment. The FMCS environment includes Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform, Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI, Dataverse, LMS365, Azure Government / GCC Medium, custom .NET/C# applications, SQL Server, PostgreSQL where applicable, Collabspace Enterprise Records Management, CCMS, Arbitration systems, the FMCS public web environment, AWS-hosted workloads, and legacy systems.

The scope includes project management, operations and maintenance, minor enhancements, major enhancements, application development, modernization and migration support, cloud migration support, cybersecurity compliance support, ATO/POA&M support, Section 508/accessibility support, records management support, documentation, reporting, quality control, transition support, and related technical services.

The BPA will include the following service CLINs:

CLIN 0001 Project Management
CLIN 0002 Operations and Maintenance and Minor Enhancements
CLIN 0003 Major Enhancements, Modernization, and Migration
The Government anticipates issuing Initial BPA Call 0001 concurrently with BPA award for Base Period ADSS Services. The anticipated BPA period of performance consists of one 12-month base period and three 12-month option periods. The estimated BPA ceiling is $4,500,000. The BPA does not obligate funds; funds will be obligated only through BPA Calls issued by the Contracting Officer.

This RFQ is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside under GSA MAS SIN 54151S. Only quotes submitted by GSA Sch
opps.summary:

The agency operates a cloud-based enterprise digital environment centered on Microsoft 365, supporting a geographically distributed workforce across the United States. USCIS has a requirement for Continuous Monitoring & Agile Security Services (CMASS).  USCIS seeks to establish a more sustainable and effective continuous monitoring program, leveraging data from security and governance tools to enhance our ability to identify and mitigate cybersecurity risks, evaluate emerging technologies, streamline security-related tasks. Additionally, we seek to transform the Ongoing Authorization (OA) program to incorporate new capabilities and risk models for Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) and implement appropriate governance measures. The CMASS resources will continue to support security authorization and continuous monitoring activities, the Vulnerability Management & Configuration Management Programs, role-based cybersecurity training, and system stakeholder outreach to ensure USCIS meets security and DHS Information System Performance Plan (ISPP) requirements.

opps.title: Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS)
opps.description: The combined USCIS Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS) project will deliver secure, scalable, and modern IT solutions supporting data analytics, machine learning, entity resolution, biometrics and identity management, and scheduling for the nationâ€™s immigration system. Leveraging agile methodologies and advanced Development, Security, and Operations (DevSecOps) practices, multi-disciplinary teams develop, operate, and maintain cloud-based applications using a robust suite of open-source and commercial tools. The project emphasizes automation, continuous integration and delivery, and strict adherence to federal security, privacy, and accessibility standards. Teams are responsible for end-to-end development, operations, security, and technical support, ensuring high-quality code, rapid incident response, and comprehensive documentation. This integrated approach enables USCIS to efficiently and securely manage complex identity and biometrics systems for both internal and public-facing applications.

opps.title: Sources Sought - HHS and OS Digital Accessibility Program Services
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Financial Resources
opps.description:
This is a SMALL BUSINESS SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE (hereafter referred to as “Notice”) issued for the purpose of planning and market research. This is NOT a solicitation for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotations and this does not obligate the Office of Mission Acquisition Solutions (OMAS) to award a contract now or in the future.
THIS IS STRICTLY MARKET RESEARCH. No reimbursement will be made for any costs associated with providing information in response to this announcement and any follow-up information requests. Respondents will not be notified of the results of the capability assessment. The Government will not entertain questions regarding the results of this market research, and requests for additional information will not be honored. All information submitted in response to this announcement must arrive on or before the closing date. The Government reserves the right to use any submitted data in a resultant solicitation.

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 25-50 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@st

opps.title: Integrating Technology into the Delivery of Clinical Care at Health Centers
opps.description:
The purpose of this initiative is to advance the integration of technology, digital innovation (through AI and patient wearable devices) to support more efficient chronic disease prevention and control, with a focus on hypertension, diabetes, and obesity, across HRSA-funded health centers and to reduce health center administrative burden. 
Wearable devices, including smartwatches, rings, fitness trackers, and continuous glucose monitors (CGMs), generate continuous patient health data that, when securely integrated into electronic health record (EHR) systems, equip providers with timely, actionable insights to improve clinical decision-making, close gaps in care, and support value-based reimbursement. 
This contract will also incorporate sister agency initiatives, where applicable, including the Center for Medicare & Medicaid Service's (CMS's) Advancing Chronic Care with Effective Scalable Solutions (ACCESS), Food and Drug Administration's (FDA's) Technology-Enabled Meaningful Patient Outcomes (TEMPO) for Digital Health Devices Pilot, and the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology (ONC) interoperability standards, including the United States Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) and USCDI+ guidance to complement the five pillars of the HHS AI Strategy.
By improving digital and health literacy for both patients and providers, this initiative centers on a patient-focused approach; empowering patients, strengthening shared decision-making, and aligning with HHS's Making America Healthy Again (MAHA) initiative. Through tailored technical assistance, peer learning, and evaluation, this program will help health centers implement strategies that measurably improve outcomes for patients and communities across the country.

opps.title: FTCA Compliant Risk Management Certification Program
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to enable HRSA to fulfill its statutory obligations under 42 U.S.C. 233 by strengthening patient safety, risk management, and operational consistency across FTCA-deemed health centers through a standardized, nationally recognized training and certification program leveraging an existing solution tailored to HRSA needs. This effort will establish clear and enforceable competency benchmarks for risk management professionals, ensuring alignment with FTCA requirements and federal expectations for oversight, accountability, and risk mitigation.
The program will promote consistency in risk management practices, including documentation, credentialing oversight, and claims analysis, improving the defensibility of clinical care and reducing avoidable malpractice exposure to the Federal Government. By equipping staff with the necessary knowledge and skills, it will enhance compliance, strengthen risk identification and mitigation, and support a culture of patient safety, ultimately standardizing risk management capabilities and reducing variability across the Health Center Program.
To establish and deliver a comprehensive, standardized healthcare risk management training and certification solution that leverages an existing, nationally recognized certification program, tailored to meet HRSA and FTCA requirements, and equips health center and free clinic staff in ambulatory care settings with the knowledge and proficiency to effectively manage risk. This initiative supports HRSA's statutory responsibilities under 42 U.S.C. 233 by building core competencies to enable participants to lead, implement, and sustain risk management programs; improving consistency in documentation, credentialing oversight, and risk analysis; and strengthening compliance with FTCA requirements. opps.title: Avaya PBX Phone System Replacement for Glacier National Park
opps.description:
Glacier National Park (GLAC) currently operates two legacy Avaya/Nortel CS1000 Private Branch Exchange (PBX) systems interconnected via T1 trunks between the Park Headquarters in West Glacier and the Saint Mary Visitor Center complex. The existing infrastructure supports approximately 300 IP/VoIP endpoints (Avaya 1120E handsets), 150 analog lines (serving fax, alarm, and OPX circuits extending up to 24 miles), and voicemail services for approximately 400 mailboxes.

The current CS1000 platform has reached end-of-life (EOL) and end-of-support (EOS) status, so the purpose of this requirement is to procure and install an Avaya IP Office platform at the Headquarters and Saint Mary locations, including 300 Avaya J179 IP phone handsets, software licensing, auto attendant/phone tree and voicemail configuration, 3-year maintenance support, and professional installation services.

This IS a brand name requirement.

This IS a total small business set-aside.

Contract Type: The contract awarded as a result of this RFQ will be a firm-fixed price commercial contract.

Questions are due at least 5 BUSINESS DAYS before the quote due date, making them due by COB 7/21/2026 at the latest.

NOTE: Your company must also be registered in System for Award Management (SAM) at the time of award and throughout the entire award/contract performance - The registration must be "active" in SAM.gov. - Government will award to the technically acceptable offer from a responsible offeror, that represents the best value to the government

*** BE ADVISED - THE PRICE IN GSA eBUY WILL BE THE EVALUATED PRICE ***
CONTRACTING POC: Kiara Stewart - kiara_stewart@nps.gov

The goal of this contract is to procure and install a new Avaya IP Office platform to ensure continued reliable communication services at both the Headquarters in West Glacier and the Saint Mary Visitor Center complex.

opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description: The contractor will be responsible for a comprehensive range of services to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the USCG Financial Service Center's operations. This involves the full lifecycle of automation, from analyzing current financial accounting processes to designing and developing new automated solutions using tools such as the Microsoft 365 Power Platform, UiPath, and SharePoint. Alongside development, the contractor must provide continuous sustainment and operational support for all automated systems, which includes monitoring, administration, troubleshooting, and user assistance to ensure their reliability. Furthermore, the role requires supporting government-led initiatives to standardize and improve business processes by mapping workflows, identifying control gaps, and creating a library of documentation like Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), in addition to developing and delivering training programs. Finally, the contractor will handle general support tasks, including the creation of detailed project documentation, reporting on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to measure the impact of automation, and managing communications with all stakeholders regarding system updates and enhancements.

opps.title: Ohio National Guard Marketing & Advertising Services
opps.description:
The Ohio Army National Guard (OHARNG) is seeking sources for marketing and advertising services for their Recruiting & Retention Battalion (RRB). The objective of this requirement is to establish a contractor-supported digital marketing and lead management ecosystem that enables the OHARNG RRB to modernize recruiting operations by consolidating online recruiting presence, implementing call tracking and routing visibility, replacing Excel-based lead tracking with a CRM, coordinating paid media administration, delivering quarterly market research, automating cold lead nurturing, and providing performance reporting for Government oversight.

The purpose of this announcement is to gain knowledge of potential sources for these services including interested Small Business sources. This notice is for planning purposes only. All interested parties should respond. The Ohio National Guard intends to issue a solicitation and award of a contract for these services.

All interested concerns will provide this office, in writing, with a notice stating their positive intent to submit a proposal no later than July 27th, 2026. This notification, which shall not exceed five typewritten pages, must include (1) positive statement of intent to bid as a prime contractor, and (2) A listing of similar services provided over the past three years, both for government and private industry. The type of service, dollar value, Contract number, location, and Point of Contact are to be included. 

In the event adequate Small Business contractors are not available for adequate competition for this project, it may be advertised as unrestricted. Information shall be provided via email to:
Primary: Claudia Tenaglia, Contracting Specialist, claudia.j.tenaglia.civ@army.mil
Alternate: Herbert Brown, Contracting Officer, herbert.l.brown24.civ@army.mil

Include the following in the subject line "Request for Information (RFI)# 1823475. No facsimile responses will be honored.

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), through its Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization, aims to provide enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities. This initiative is designed to enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems, reducing duplication and lowering costs while accelerating modernization efforts.

The D&PE API Services organization seeks contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain critical middleware applications and shared services

The purpose of this requirement is to modernize a legacy platform delivering priority telecommunications services and to provide operations and maintenance support for applications supporting the infrastructure protection and risk analysis mission spaces. These systems support CISAâ€™s mission to understand, manage, and reduce risk to the nationâ€™s critical cyber and physical infrastructure. CISA infrastructure analysis informs decisions made by DHS and public and private sector partners and assists CISA in framing policy and programs, prioritizing its operational activities and maximizing operational effectiveness through more efficient use of resources.

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to, modernization of existing platforms to leverage Software as a Service (SaaS) and cloud native services; cloud-hosted mission system administration; information assurance management; certification and accreditation support; software patches and upgrades; configuration management; user management and support; backup and recovery; and performance monitoring.  This requirement does not include enterprise commodity IT operations, enterprise desktop support, enterprise network operations, or generalized agency-wide IT service desk support. 

This action will be competed and awarded against the multiple award Digital Transformation Support Services (DTSS) Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA)

opps.title: Cyber Grant Support and Program Development
opps.description:
Summary of SOW Requirements: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), via the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Integrated Operations Division (IOD), requires the Contractor to deliver comprehensive support for the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) and Tribal Cybersecurity Grant Program (TCGP). The Contractor will actively advance program development, maintain information continuity, and ensure operational excellence across CISA regions, fostering strong coordination with CISA Divisions, Mission Enabling Offices, and interagency partners.

Scope and Objectives: The Contractor is responsible for both administrative and programmatic support to IOD, facilitating communication, capacity building, and awareness of cybersecurity grant programs. The Contractor will coordinate efforts across CISA regions and headquarters, strengthen regional capabilities, support service delivery, and execute IOD priorities.

Key Tasks and Requirements:
1. Program Support and Development: Collect and analyze best practices, lessons learned, and requirements to drive continuous improvement and inform future grant opportunities. Provide relevant documents and facilitate delivery of grant programs across regions, enhancing cyber competency among security advisors, analysts, and outreach staff. Deliver training to regional staff, ensuring readiness and effective execution. Establish mechanisms to capture return on investment (ROI) through program monitoring, and manage technical assistance for Cybersecurity Advisors and State Coordinators. Support annual program monitoring and grant close-out activities.

2. Operational and Strategic Engagement: Maintain and enhance tools such as the Service Change Request Tool and IOD Intake Process to gather requirements, prioritize services, and transition to full operating capability. Improve dashboards and automate reporting, including integration with ServiceNow. Develop engagement plans and public-facing material

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.description:
PRESOLICITATION NOTICE

Notice ID: 89243126RSC000172

1. Contracting Organization


	Agency: U.S. Department of Energy (DOE)
	Office: Office of Science (SC)


2. Set-Aside Status


	Set-Aside: 8(a) Competitive (in accordance with RFO Part 19 set-aside authorities)


3. NAICS Code

NAICS: 541690—Other Scientific and Technical Consulting Services (Small Business Size Standard: $19.0 million)

4. Description of Requirements The contractor provides safeguards and security (S&S) technical and administrative support services. These services assist the Office of Safety and Security (OSS), the Consolidated Service Center (CSC), and the Oak Ridge Office of Environmental Management (OREM) in Oak Ridge, Tennessee.

The contractor performs the following core tasks:

Audit and Survey Support: Conducts on-site S&S compliance surveys, audits, and self-assessments.

Access Vetting and Credentials: Processes approximately 11,000 active access authorizations, evaluates public trust cases, and issues badges/credentials under HSPD-12 and Trusted Workforce 2.0 standards.

Information and Technical Protection: Reviews documents for derivative classification, declassification, and Unclassified Controlled Nuclear Information (UCNI) parameters. Supports Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI), Operations Security (OPSEC), and Technical Security (COMSEC, TSCM, TEMPEST) programs.

Budgeting and Metrics Formulation: Implements activity-based costing models, reviews budget data submissions, and tracks security performance metrics.

Industrial Security Support: Analyzes Foreign Ownership, Control, or Influence (FOCI) submissions and updates facility clearances in the SSIMS database.

Administrative Hearings Support: Coordinates administrative reviews, compiles hearing exhibit books, and schedules court reporter services.

opps.title: Equinix Data Center Services Renewal
opps.description:
DHS/S&T/CIO requires renewal of Data Center cage space, power, cooling and connectivity for 10 cabinets at Equinix Ashburn and associated wiring and zero hop network connections and internet connection services to DHS Homeland Security Enterprise Network (HSEN), other carriers, and Cloud Service Providers (CSP)s.  The DHS HSEN cage is located in Equinix Ashburn, VA, Bldg DC11.  The DHS S&T Equinix cage also is located at Equinix, Ashburn, Va, Bldg DC11.  
The Data Center Service Provider shall provide the infrastructure support for a Government Furnished Equipment (GFE) Level 1-Basic Services solution.  The Contractor shall continue to provide a colocation facility support for the DHS S&T core infrastructure. The requested facility is located in the DC Metropolitan area that is within fifty 50 mile radius of DHS S&T location (3701 Martin Luther King Ave, SW, Washington DC 20528). The facility shall provide direct zero hop access to DHS HSEN and to carriers such as AT&T, Verizon and Level 1 and CSPs such as Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google, Oracle, and IBM. This direct connection facilitates better access and lower latency to applications within the above-mentioned entities.  Zero hop is defined as providing direct, physical connectivity within the colocation facility campus to HSEN, CSPs, Tier 1 Internet Gateways, and Telcos. This is required to maintain system functionality by reducing network hops and latency, which are caused due to the numerous interfaces between DHS systems, to a minimum. Placing the DHS S&T network systems in a co-location facility also facilitates the routing and inspection of traffic between CSPs, as well as, between the internal DHS S&T network to HSEN and to the CSPs. Additionally, DHS S&T requires space within the Washington DC metropolitan area and direct patching to DHS HSENâ€™s cage. These specialized requirements can only be met by Equinix.

opps.title: MGD Project Management and Strategy Support
opps.description:
This requirement seeks comprehensive Project Management and Strategy Support services for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection's (CBP) Office of Information and Technology (OIT), Management and Governance Directorate (MGD). The primary goal is to enhance OIT's operational effectiveness, improve the transparency of strategic planning and governance, and ensure the successful execution and alignment of strategic initiatives.

The contractor will provide essential support for both project management and strategic planning functions. This includes comprehensive oversight and control of all project activities, managing technical resources, quality assurance, scheduling, cost, and financial controls to ensure deliverables are completed on time, within budget, and to required quality standards. The contractor will be responsible for planning, directing, controlling, measuring, monitoring, and reporting on all project activities, submitting monthly status reports and quarterly performance reviews according to Government-specified templates. Additionally, the contractor will furnish program management tools, guidance, plans, processes, procedures, and resources to personnel.

Concurrently, the contractor will provide critical strategy management support to mature stakeholder relationships, align strategic initiatives, and facilitate the development and implementation of OIT's IT Strategic Plan. This involves supporting the CBP Chief Information Officer's (CIO) Trusted Partner Program and relationship management dashboards, with efforts to formalize these relationships across DHS, other government agencies, and industry. Key activities include collecting, managing, and curating strategic-level data for analysis and reporting, supporting the development of IT policy documents and dissemination platforms, and enhancing/maintaining the Strategic Traceability Hub and Power BI-based data visualizations, ensuring 99% uptime and timely dashboard enhancements. Technical development

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source Peace Corps' Marketing Campaign 2026-2029
opps.agency: Peace Corps Office of Acquisition and Contract Management
opps.description:
THIS IS A NOTICE OF INTENT TO SOLE SOURCE, NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTES (RFQ). 

The United States Peace Corps, Office of the Chief Financial Officer, Acquisition and Contract Management (OCFO/ACM), intends to award a sole source, logical follow-on Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) under FAR 8.405-6(a)(1)(i)(C) for Marketing, Communications, Website, and Advertising Production Services.

This requirement supports the Peace Corps’ national-level Integrated Marketing Campaign and is essential to maintaining continuity of creative development, production, and deployment of mission critical outreach activities and volunteer recruitment. The planned anticipated BPA includes a base year and two one-year option periods, with an estimated total value of $30 million. All work shall be performed in strict accordance with the current Performance Work Statement and shall be subject to the terms and conditions of the GSA Schedule contract. 

Under the current BPA, the incumbent contractor is developing the approved 2027 national campaign. If the agency were to compete the follow-on BPA, any new contractor would be required to undergo 6–12 months of onboarding and discovery in advance of campaign development, resulting in unacceptable delays, substantial duplication of cost, and significant disruption to national outreach and recruitment activities. The Peace Corps is pursuing the strategic goal of placing 8,000 Volunteers by 2030. Continuity of national marketing operations is essential to achieving this objective. 

NO RFQ WILL BE ISSUED AND QUOTES WILL NOT BE REQUESTED. Capability statements will not be accepted.

opps.title: Financial Management and Business Support Services
opps.description:
Sources Sought Notice - #2032H326N00011
Financial Management and Business Support Services

1.0	SYNOPSIS
The Treasury Common Services Center (TCSC) provides shared acquisition and operational support services across the Department of the Treasury (Treasury). As part of this mission, TCSC Acquisition Services supports Treasury-wide procurement reporting, analytics, data quality, and acquisition oversight activities.

The U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of the Deputy Chief Financial Officer (ODCFO) requires specialized financial analysis, accounting, and business services to support its business operations.  These services will help improve operational efficiencies, systems and data integrity, and support the implementation of new and current agency-wide business initiatives. Responses to this Sources Sought Notice (SSN) will help ODCFO assess the marketplace for contractors capable of delivering the required services.

The contractor shall provide financial management and accounting support services to include technical research of GAAP, OMB, and legislative requirements, accounting and analytical services in areas such as proprietary and budgetary reporting, variance analysis, problem resolution, data reconciliation, abnormal accounting conditions and business process improvements while also helping to maintain robust internal controls that facilitate Treasury's compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and guidance.  The Contractor must provide all management, labor, and supervision necessary to satisfy Performance Work Statement (PWS) requirements.

opps.title: CDC Artificial Intelligence for Acquisition Support Reverse Industry Day
opps.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
CDC is interested in learning about existing Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools that can support the federal acquisition lifecycle, including acquisition planning, market research, requirements development, solicitation preparation, proposal evaluation, contract administration, and closeout. CDC plans to host a limited number of companies to demonstrate commercially available AI capabilities to a select audience of government acquisition professionals. The purpose of these presentations is to increase awareness of current AI solutions that may improve the efficiency, effectiveness, and quality of acquisition processes.

These presentations are for market research purposes only and are intended solely to inform the Government of current commercial capabilities. They do not constitute a solicitation or imply any future procurement action.

Vendors will be expected to attend a virtual session to present their existing Artificial Intelligence (AI) tool(s), with approximately 30 minutes allocated to the presentation and 15 minutes allocated to questions and answers.  Audiences will be limited to government acquisition staff.

CDC estimates the event will be scheduled in August, but does not currently have a specific date set.  

Interested vendors shall submit a capability statement of no more than two (2) pages to Lauren Peel at ijt9@cdc.gov.  The subject line must be: 75D301-26-RFI-73483 [Vendor Name].

Capability statements shall reference the specific tool(s) or capabilities available and discuss whether currently used by other agencies or commercial companies or whether still in development.  The capability statement should identify the specific acquisition issues or concerns being addressed.   

Questions may be submitted by July 20, 2026, at 5PM EST.  Answers to select questions will be posted by amendment to this announcement.  Responses are due July 30, 2026. 

Vendors will receive a confirmation as to whether or not they are selected to present.

opps.title: A Comparison of Health Care Use and Mortality among Dual Eligibles
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration
opps.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office of the Assistant Secretary for Planning and Evaluation (ASPE), Office of Mission Acquisition Solutions (OMAS) has a requirement for: "A Comparison of Health Care Use and Mortality Among Dual Eligibles in C-SNPs and I-SNPs to Those in Other Medicare Advantage Plans" in accordance with Section C Program Description and Background.  

The Contractor shall independently provide all personnel, labor, facilities, materials, equipment, supplies, and services necessary to perform the Statement of Work, except those expressly identified as Government-furnished.

The study will conduct two quantitative analyses: the first comparing outcomes for dually eligible individuals in Chronic Condition Special Needs Plans (C-SNPs) to those in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and regular Medicare Advantage (MA) plans; the second comparing outcomes for those in Institutional Special Needs Plans (I-SNPs) to those in D-SNPs and regular MA plans. 

The work includes project management, data acquisition through Data Use Agreements (DUAs), development of a detailed work plan and data analysis plan, rigorous data analysis using various government data sources, and the production of research briefs and presentations suitable for policy audiences. 

The Federal Supply Schedule Category "Professional Services," Special Item Number: 541611 and the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code: 541611 "Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services" are applicable to this requirement.  The Product Service Code is: R799 (Other Management Support Services).

opps.title: Sources Sought-FTCA-Compliant Risk Management Certification Program
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to enable HRSA to fulfill its statutory obligations under 42 U.S.C. 233 by strengthening patient safety, risk management, and operational consistency across FTCA-deemed health centers through a standardized, nationally recognized training and certification program leveraging an existing solution tailored to HRSA needs. This effort will establish clear and enforceable competency benchmarks for risk management professionals, ensuring alignment with FTCA requirements and federal expectations for oversight, accountability, and risk mitigation.
The program will promote consistency in risk management practices, including documentation, credentialing oversight, and claims analysis, improving the defensibility of clinical care and reducing avoidable malpractice exposure to the Federal Government. By equipping staff with the necessary knowledge and skills, it will enhance compliance, strengthen risk identification and mitigation, and support a culture of patient safety, ultimately standardizing risk management capabilities and reducing variability across the Health Center Program.

opps.title: Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC)
opps.agency: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
opps.description: The Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC) program is a CMS initiative designed to safeguard the integrity and accuracy of payments made to Medicare Advantage (MA) plans. As the primary mechanism through which CMS validates payment accuracy, the RADV program relies on this contractor to perform and support a comprehensive suite of medical record review activities. These activities include the intake of medical records, abstraction of diagnosis codes, quality assurance and continuous improvement efforts, appeals support, and systems and reporting enhancements all of which work together to ensure that MA plan payments are based on accurate and verified clinical documentation. This task order is issued under the Center for Program Integrity (CPI) Payment Accuracy Acquisition Strategy, reflecting CMS's broader commitment to program integrity and responsible stewardship of federal healthcare funds.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
After the completion of the State Laws digest on illegal passing, a more in-depth review or state of knowledge report is needed to determine if a model law for illegal passing would help with the consistency of enforcement of these driving violations. More clarity if needed on how currently laws are enforced to help better inform states to improve enforcement of these laws. The state laws digest informed that laws vary in content, organization, consistency, and clarity. Also, that what may constitute illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neigh-boring one. States also vary in their methods to review, document and report violations including permissibility of stop-arm cameras. This report is needed to give clarity on how states can best improve enforcement of this law.

To address this need, this project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This study will provide if a model law would be necessary for continuity among enforcement within the U.S States and provide best example of a model law.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is seeking to enhance the enforcement of laws against the illegal passing of school buses, a significant safety concern for students. 

The project aims to address inconsistencies in enforcement and prosecution across various jurisdictions, as current laws vary widely in their content and clarity. A previous digest indicated that illegal passing violations are often dismissed in court, highlighting the need for improved education and advocacy among judicial officials. opps.title: Updated: Enterprise Remote Document Authentication
opps.description:
*** 5/19/2026: USCIS anticipates releasing a solicitation on NASA SEWP V set-aside to small business and anticipates solicitation release in early June. This requirement will be competed without any brand-name specifications.***

USCIS anticipate a requirement for a commercial software solution for document authentication and identity verification service. These services are essential forÂ maintainingÂ program integrity, supporting federal operations, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Historically, many USCIS processes have relied on manual, in-person document review, which can present operational challenges and limit access and flexibility,Â especially for remote or distributed users. More specifically, the solution will capture high quality images of identity and work authorization documents, apply Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) and face match checks, use template and barcode/MRZ based authentication, and extract structured data via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for use in USCIS-controlled microservices. All processing will occur within USCIS or approved Department of Homeland Security (DHS) environments, in compliance with DHS security and privacy requirements.

opps.description:
July 7, 2026: This is Amendment 0009 to solicitation 47QRCA23R0002.

The General Services Administration (GSA), Federal Acquisition Service (FAS), Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD), is issuing Amendment 0009 in accordance with RFO-2025-05 Supplement 1 FAR 5.101 for the One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+), Indefinite-Delivery, Indefinite-Quantity (IDIQ), Multi-Agency Contracts (MAC). The System for Award Management (SAM.gov) is the official source for information about this procurement.

The following documents and attachments are updated as a result of Amendment 0009:


	OASIS+ 8(a) RFP (current as of Amendment 0009)
	SF30 (Amendment 0009) and Continuation Sheet


Provisional Attachments


	J.P-1 OASIS+ Domain Qualifications Matrix and Scorecards
	J.P-2 FPDS Sample
	J.P-3 Project Verification Form
	J.P-4 Domains Auto-Relevant NAICS Codes and PSCs
	J.P-9 Cost/Price Template
	J.P-14 Lateral Springboarding Summary Sheet


Contract Attachments


	J-2 Task Order, Transactional Data, and Contract Access Fee Reporting
	J-4 Department of Defense Required Provisions and Clauses for Task Orders
	J-5 Task Order Clause and Provision Matrix


Consistent with Section H.11 of the OASIS+ RFP, GSA intends to maintain continuously open solicitations to support rolling admissions/on-ramping and to allow existing awardees, when eligible, to compete for additional Domains as Government needs evolve. Should GSA determine a need to close any domain or solicitation, the agency will provide public notification. Offerors are permitted to submit a single proposal for multiple domains under a single solicitation. There is no fixed timeline for award; timelines will vary based on the volume of proposals received and are subject to resource availability.

Offerors must be registered and active in SAM.gov. All proposals must be submitted through the OASIS+ SubmissionPortal (OSP). The OSP is open for registration and submission of offers as of January 12,

opps.title: Information Technology Support Services (ITSS)
opps.agency: DHS Office of the Chief Procurement Officer
opps.description:
This is a notice of intent to issue a modification to extend the performance period of task order 70RSAT25FC0000007 for Information Technology Support Services (ITSS) issued against Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) 70RSAT22A00000001 with Silosmashers, Inc., 12001 Sunrise Valley Drive, Suite 202, Reston, VA 20191.  The task order will be modified to extend performance for 10 months on a non-competitive basis. The statutory authority permitting other than full and open competition is 41 U.S.C. 3304(a)(1) implemented by the FAR Subpart 6.103-1 (FAR Class Deviation 25-11) entitled “Only One Responsible Source and No Other Supplies or Services Will Satisfy Agency Requirements.”   

Background 

This modification to existing task order 70RSAT25FC0000007 for Information Technology Support Services (ITSS) will maintain support services to Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science & Technology Directorate (S&T) Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) in the areas of program management, business management, enterprise architecture alignment, IT governance, and regulatory compliance for platforms such as SharePoint, ServiceNow, and Microsoft Power Platform.  The task order includes labor, travel, and materials necessary to assist DHS S&T OCIO fulfill its mission.  The task order has a period of performance that began on September 30, 2025, and will expire on July 31, 2026, unless extended. The market research for the re-compete of 70RSAT25FC0000007 is under development. Due to the lapse in DHS appropriations in FY26, preparations for the re-compete have been significantly delayed.

Silosmashers is the current contractor for the requirement, and they possess unique and specialized knowledge of DHS S&T CIO requirements, activities, and stakeholders to ensure seamless, continuous support during the acquisition activities required for the re-compete. Efforts to conduct a competitive procurement and then potentially transition to a new contrac

opps.title: eAMLIS Operations and Maintenance and CYBERSECURITY
opps.agency: Office of the Secretary of the Interior
opps.description:
The U.S. Department of the Interior, Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement (OSMRE), seeks quotations from GSA MAS contractors for continued Operations & Maintenance (O&M), ETL/GIS workflow support, system enhancements, legislative/policy update integration, cybersecurity compliance, and transition planning for the Enhanced Abandoned Mine Land Inventory System (eAMLIS).
eAMLIS is a mature, mission-critical system supporting statutory obligations under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act (SMCRA). It provides annually reported AML inventory information to DOI leadership, Congress, States, Tribes, and external stakeholders. System requirements, technical objectives, and operational responsibilities are detailed in the attached Statement of Objectives (SOO).

The Contractor shall provide all personnel, tools, equipment, and services necessary to maintain stable system operations, support ETL pipelines and geospatial features, execute modernization efforts, and ensure timely updates in response to legislative changes and federal audits (including OIG recommendations).

A hybrid Firm-Fixed-Price (FFP) and Labor-Hour (LH) order is anticipated.

The Government intends to award a task order from this solicitation to the responsible offeror whose offer conforming to the solicitation will be most advantageous to the Government, price and other factors considered. The following factors shall be used to evaluate offers: 
Factor 1: Technical Approach (Most Important)
Factor 2: Relevant Experience & Past Performance
Factor 3: Price

The Government will select the best-suited vendor using a comparative evaluation process IAW FAR 80405-2(c)(3). 

Please see attached solicitation (B06 - SOL-eAMLIS) for full RFQ details.

opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
Notice of Intent to Sole Source

HT941026N0060



THIS IS A NOTICE OF INTENT TO AWARD A SOLE SOURCE SUPPLY CONTRACT AND IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE QUOTES. The Defense Health Agency Contracting Activity (DHACA), Healthcare Contracting Division – West (HCD-W), San Diego, intends to negotiate and award a firm fixed price contract for Data Innovations Instrument Manager Licenses and Drivers for all analyzers used at Armed Services Blood Donor Center (ASBBC) in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation Overhaul (RFO) 12.102(a), for purchases valued at or below the simplified acquisition threshold, only one source reasonably available to:



Locked Core Tech, LLC (Mission First Solutions LLC)

18304 Cabin RD

Triangle, Virginia 22172



The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this requirement is 541511 - Custom Computer Programming Services; the business size standard is $34.0M. The Product Service Code (PSC) is DA01 - IT and Telecom - Business Application/application Development Support Services (labor).



The objective of this sole source award is to procure Data Innovations Instrument Manager (DI IM) middleware platform for the Armed Services Blood Bank Center (ASBBC) located at U. S. Naval Hospital Okinawa (USNHO), Japan.



The requirement is to procure and implement the Data Innovations Instrument Manager (DI IM) middleware through Locked Core Tech LLC (formally Mission First Solutions LLC) to establish automated bidirectional interfaces between five infectious disease testing analyzers at the Armed Services Blood Bank Center (ASBBC) Okinawa and the Enterprise Blood Management System – Donor (EBMS-D). The DI IM middleware will automate transmission of test orders, specimen data, quality control information, and finalized results between laboratory instruments and EBMS-D, eliminating manual data entry and reducing the risk of transcription and reporting errors. The solution will improve workflow efficiency, enhance d

opps.title: DHS CUBE
opps.description:
REQUEST FOR INFORMATION. THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY.  
This RFI is for planning purposes only and shall not be construed as an obligation on the part of the Government.  This is NOT a Request for Quotations or Proposals.  No solicitation document exists, and a formal solicitation may or may not be issued by the Government as a result of the responses received to this RFI.  The Government will not pay for any response or demonstration expenses.  Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary or competition sensitive information contained in their response.  Responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract.  Those who respond to this RFI should not anticipate feedback concerning their submission.  All submissions will become the property of the Government and will not be returned.  Responding to the RFI is completely voluntary and will not enhance or adversely affect responses to any resulting solicitation if such solicitation is issued at a later date.  There is no competitive advantage gained by any of the respondents to this RFI.

BACKGROUND

The DHS Office of the Chief Readiness Support Officer (OCRSO) requires contract support for the DHS Cube, which is an information technology tool that integrates the Department's financial, acquisition, human capital, contracting, assets, and security data to support analysis and decision making. Through the DHS Cube, the Department is creating an integrated structure that allows data to be utilized more efficiently in scenarios ranging from day-to-day activities to incident management decision-making by the Department's senior-most officials.
The objective is to provide information technology (IT), software development, and business functional support associated with the operations, maintenance, enhancement, maturation, and data population of the DHS Cube system and capabilities. The DHS Cube is a Microsoft Azure data warehouse

opps.description:
This notice is issued by the USPFO-NM Contracting Office solely for market research and planning purposes. It does not constitute a Request for Quotation (RFQ) or a promise to issue an RFQ in the future. This request for information does not commit the Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. The Government will not reimburse any costs incurred by vendors in the preparation of information submitted in response to this notice.

1. Introduction & Purpose
The New Mexico Air National Guard (NMANG) is seeking to identify businesses, particularly small businesses, capable of providing a comprehensive sports marketing and sponsorship package. The purpose of this sponsorship is to improve the visibility and community footprint of the NMANG, increase brand awareness, generate qualified recruiting leads, and promote military service opportunities.

The Government is seeking to determine if there are capable small businesses that can perform the required work, specifically through a partnership with the New Mexico Goatheads sports franchise or the Rio Rancho Events Center. The information received will assist the Contracting Officer in determining the appropriate acquisition strategy, which may include a small business set-aside competition.

2. Background
To effectively support its recruiting mission, the NMANG requires access to high-visibility sporting events that attract a diverse and large-scale audience throughout New Mexico. A strategic sponsorship with a premier sports franchise offers continuous exposure to thousands of spectators through venue advertising, digital media, promotional events, and fan engagement opportunities. The place of performance will be the Rio Rancho Events Center, located at 3001 Civic Center Circle, Rio Rancho, NM 87144, during New Mexico Goatheads home games and associated events.

3. Summary of Requirements
The contractor shall be required to provide a comprehensive sponsorship package that includes, but is not limited

All ICE divisions, departments and offices are authorized to utilize the services provided under this BPA, so long as the requirements are within the scope identified in the scope of this Performance Work Statement (PWS). The scope of this contract will be to provide training and communications support services for all IT investments support by the HSI IT Portfolio, and the OCIO, SDD, HSI Branch.  Training services include assessment of current program training documentation and plans to validate training needs, design and development of appropriate training materials and delivery of training for COT systems.

opps.title: Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE)
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE). This Operations and Maintenance Services requirement will assist USCIS to operate, optimize, enhance and maintain existing M365 services, onboard new M365 services, and plan and develop strategies for future service sustainment, not limited to, but including: Core productivity applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote); Collaboration and Communication (Microsoft Team, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Loop, Whiteboard); Security and Compliance (Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra ID, Intune, Rights Management and Information protection); Power Platform (Power Bi, Apps, Automate and Virtual Agents); Project and Task Management (Planner, To Do, List); Additional Services and Tools (Booking, Forms, Stream, Sway); Artificial Intelligence and Automation (Microsoft Copilot).  Assisting in planning and executing the rollout of Copilot /AI service in accordance with DHS and USCIS security and privacy policies as well as additional Microsoft 365 Services, USCIS may choose to adopt.   Additionally, USCIS needs support for M365 service integrations with both Microsoft and non-Microsoft Technologies, e.g., AirWatch, ServiceNow, Archive360, JAMF, and AgilQuest.

*Small business set aside to be determined through market research. USCIS intends to issue a Request for Information during May 2026.

opps.agency: USGS Office of Acquisitions and Grants
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description: This Sources Sought Notice is being published for the purpose of conducting Market Research to obtain information regarding the availability and capability of all qualified sources to perform a potential requirement.  This is not a solicitation.  The NEIC operates near-real-time detection algorithms on various types of streamed seismic data to improve the timeliness and accuracy of derived earthquake information products. The rapid detection algorithms are a mix of AI-driven algorithms and standard (non-AI driven) algorithms. These operational systems heavily integrate with each other to enhance derived scientific data products, with continuous seismic data acquisition systems, rapid data distribution systems, data analysis systems, data quality tools, and overall system health and monitoring systems. Additionally, with the rapid advancements of AI technologies and approaches, these detection algorithms and along with new approaches developed by seismic researchers need to be improved, modernized, and implemented to perform on continuous seismic data in 24/7 operational environments to address NEIC¿s mission critical requirements of timeliness and accuracy. NEIC Software needs focused on artificial intelligence/machine learning (AI/ML) enhancements for real-time, continuous, seismic data streams and detections and algorithmic monitoring include the following software development tasks:1. Develop and Maintain Real-Time Seismic Data Streaming and Detection Systems2. Modernize and Optimize Detection Algorithms3. Integrate and Advance AI/ML Capabilities4. Build and Integrate Monitoring, Archiving, and Distribution Systems5. opps.title: International Procurement Experts
opps.description:
The Millennium Challenge Corporation (MCC), Program Acquisition & Assistance Practice Group (PAA) within the Department of Compact Operations (DCO) seeks to award contract with a firm to provide multiple procurement experts on an as-needed basis with extensive international procurement experience under donor funded projects, such as funded by the World Bank, MCC, Asian Development Bank etc. These experts will support MCC PAA in developing procurement policy, guidance, and tools for use by MCC partner country Accountable Entities (MCAs) in their procurement operations and assist MCC PAA in measuring performance of those operations and recommending improvements in procedures and application of tools. The Contractor will assist MCC PAA in guiding the MCAs and their procurement agents through providing training, advice, hands-on support on MCC's procurement policies, procedures, guidance and standard bidding documents, procurement-related technologies, market outreach efforts, and helping them in overseeing the compliance thereto to meet MCC requirements. Extensive interaction with MCC will be required for the experts to fully understand MCC's procurement documents and/or to advise MCC in modifying these documents as and when needed.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

opps.title: Army Contract Writing System (ACWS) Sustainment, Support, and Enhancement
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The Army Contracting Command – Rock Island (ACC-RI), on behalf of the Product Manager (PdM) for the Army Contract Writing System (ACWS), is seeking feedback from industry on capabilities to sustain, modernize, and integrate contract writing and administration solutions. These solutions will support the Army's acquisition community by automating the contracting lifecycle, modernizing legacy systems, and integrating intelligent automation.

The Government is interested in solutions from all sources, including commercial, government, traditional, and non-traditional defense contractors. We are specifically seeking innovative approaches to implement Secure Environments (IL-5/IL-6), merge Virtual Contracting Enterprise (VCE) capabilities, and proactively integrate Agentic AI and machine learning. While the current ACWS utilizes an Appian foundation, the Government is taking a platform-agnostic approach and encourages solutions that optimize performance and prevent vendor lock-in, whether leveraging the existing platform or proposing an alternative architecture.



The input received from this draft phase will be used to inform the evaluation criteria, technical requirements, and acquisition strategy for the Final Call for Solutions. This posting is intended to advertise the Army’s intent to solicit under the Army’s Open Solicitation (AOS) Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) via a Draft Call for Solutions. This is solely for market research and planning purposes; no proposals or formal Solution Briefs are being requested at this time.



Interested parties are directed to review the attached Draft CFS document for complete details on the program requirements.



Industry Day and Submission of Questions:
The Government will host a virtual Industry Day regarding this requirement on Thursday July 23, 2026 at 1000 CST / 1100 EST.

To streamline the collection of feedback, the registration form for this event includes a section for submitting initial quest

opps.description: PLEASE NOTE: This procurement will be procured under the 8(a) program and only businesses currently enrolled in the 8(a) program shall be considered. The Naval Surface Warfare Center, Carderock Division (NSWCCD) has a requirement to maintain the current Common Access Card (CAC) support office on site at the West Bethesda, MD location. The CAC support office allows for all military, civilian, and contractor personnel working on site, or for other DoD/Government agencies, to obtain and maintain their CACs. This requirement will contain a base year with four option years. This Sources Sought notice includes a Draft Performance Work Statement (PWS) and a Market Research Questionnaire. The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

opps.title: DCSA RFI DCSA Audio-Visual AV Design Consultant
opps.description:
The Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI) in support of the National Center for Credibility Assessment (NCCA). This RFI is issued solely for market research and planning purposes. It does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP), solicitation, or commitment by DCSA to issue an RFP or to award a contract. Responses are voluntary, nor does it obligate the Government, and will not result in reimbursement.

The National Center for Credibility Assessment (NCCA) requires a comprehensive technical assessment and engineering design effort across its instructional spaces. The core requirement is to assess the specific hardware currently installed, evaluate the physical space constraints, and verify the exact types and capabilities of the existing A/V equipment. This engineering evaluation is necessary to address system performance gaps within NCCA's current footprint, specifically regarding audio quality in 36 training labs and centralized remote viewing capabilities for 18 isolated "pods" and 2 main classrooms.

See attached RFI for details.

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source for FDA-iRISK® System for Food Safety Risk Assessment - Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) hereby issues a notice of intent in accordance with FAR 5.201. Pursuant to FAR 6.302-1, FDA will sole source with Risk Sciences International, 1505 Laperriere Avenue, Suite 343, Ottawa, ON K1Z 7T1, Canada for management and technical activities for continued system enhancements and operations and maintenance (O&M) of the FDA-iRISK capability. The FDA-iRISK capability includes the web accessible public version (FDA-iRISK 5.0) as well as the FDA-iRISK institutional version (currently version 5.1i).

As a peer-reviewed, robust food safety modeling tool that enables users to compare and rank risks from multiple microbial and chemical hazards and predict effectiveness of prevention and control measures, expressed as public-health metrics, FDA-iRISK (both the public version and the institutional version) has been and can continue to be a valuable component of our portfolio of risk assessment tools to inform FDA and stakeholder decisions related to food safety and nutrition.

Implementing this new 3-year IDIQ3 contract will allow FDA’s Human Foods Program (HFP) to continue to maintain the capability of FDA-iRISK in a continued state of readiness, ensuring its availability and usability to current and new users as a risk assessment tool for FDA regulatory and research purposes and for use by stakeholders in the scientific community, and to support FDA collaboration with other organizations using the FDA-iRISK system. Risk Sciences International is the only Contractor able to meet the requirement of this procurement. The NAICS code is 541511, Custom Computer Programming Services.

This notice is not a request for competitive quotes or proposal. No solicitation document is available and telephone requests will not be accepted. Respondents may submit information pertaining to their capabilities to perform the services required above within a timely manner at Natasha.Abbington@fda.hhs.gov, which may be considered by the agency.

opps.title: GSA - Promoting the Administration's Domestic Sourcing/Buy American Goals - MRAS
opps.description:
GSA Wants Your Feedback to Enhance Domestic Preference in GSA Acquisition Programs - Market Research

In accordance with Executive Order 14392 "Ensuring Truthful Advertising of Products Claiming to be Made in America" and in order to advance the Administration's policy to revitalize American manufacturing and promote American workers, the General Services Administration (GSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI). The RFI seeks input from industry stakeholders on how to regenerate an important part of the American economy by making it easier to buy American products through GSA Advantage. Only responses submitted through the RFI link below will be accepted. Responses submitted through any other means will NOT be evaluated or recorded.

RFI Link: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_cvEH8Pm0kAwRYfY

opps.title: 19RC1126Q0004 MEDIA MONITORING SERVICE FOR DAMASCUS-SYRIA
opps.description:
The Contractor shall provide daily, comprehensive media and social media monitoring, analysis, reporting, and dissemination services to support U.S. Government objectives and interests in Syria. The Contractor will deliver one comprehensive report per day that summarizes key narratives, provides in-depth coverage of political, social, and economic developments, and highlights emerging trends and shifts in media tone. The report shall also include monitoring and analysis of social media platforms to identify trending topics, influential actors, tonality of discussions, and message tracking relevant to Syria.

This service is critical to ensure U.S. policymakers and embassy officials receive timely, reliable, and actionable insights for informed decision-making and strategic planning in a rapidly evolving information environment.

 The Contractor will monitor a broad spectrum of media sources including, but not limited to:


	Syrian local news outlets (print, online, television, and radio).
	Syrian social media platforms (Twitter/X, Facebook, Instagram, Telegram, YouTube, TikTok, and other platforms relevant to the Syrian information space).
	Regional and international media outlets with significant coverage or influence on Syrian affairs.


The monitoring and analysis must provide U.S. Government officials with a clear understanding of the evolving media environment in Syria, identifying key narratives, trends, influencers, and disinformation campaigns impacting U.S. policy interests.

The Contractor shall provide daily, comprehensive media and social media monitoring, analysis, reporting, and dissemination services to support U.S. Government objectives and interests in Syria. opps.title: ILS Aerospace Parts Marketplace Subscription
opps.description:
INTRODUCTION

The Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWC-AD) intends to solicit and award a sole source Firm Fixed Price (FFP) purchase order to Inventory Locator service, LLC (ILS) for web-based global inventory query subscriptions for locating and purchasing aftermarket aerospace parts that are unavailable through the federal supply service and/or have long lead times from approved U.S. Government vendors. Request for Quote (RFP) Number N0042126Q1175 will be issued on a sole source basis with ILS under the authority implemented by the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 12 and 13. The Period of Performance is anticipated to be a  one year  base period with four one-year option periods.  

This notice of intent is not a request for competitive quotes. However, all responses to this notice of intent must be received no later than 12:00 pm (EST) on 21 July 2026 to Taylor Wade at taylor.a.wade7.civ@us.navy.mil.

A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed contract upon the response to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government. Information received will normally be considered for the purpose of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement. The Government will not pay for any information received.

opps.title: Integrated Workforce and Readiness Platform (ARMOR)
opps.description:
An Integrated Workforce and Readiness Platform for the Defense Health Agency

NAICS Code(s): 541511, 541512, and 541519 

1.0 Disclaimer
THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY. This RFI is being issued solely for information and planning purposes. It does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) or a promise to issue an RFP in the future. This request for information does not commit the Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. Further, the Defense Health Agency (DHA) is not at this time seeking proposals and will not accept unsolicited proposals. Respondents are advised that the U.S. Government will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in response to this RFI; all costs associated with responding to this RFI will be solely at the interested party’s expense. Not responding to this RFI does not preclude participation in any future RFP, if any is issued.

2.0 Background
The Defense Health Agency (DHA) is responsible for managing a complex, global healthcare enterprise. A primary challenge is the fragmentation of critical workforce data across distinct, standalone platforms. Manpower systems track billet assignments, credentialing systems manage clinical privileging, and separate databases monitor medical clearances, operational skillsets (JKSAs), and other position specific training. Because these systems lack native interoperability, leadership must manually reconcile who is assigned with what they are authorized, medically cleared, and operationally ready to perform.

This architectural disconnect introduces unacceptable risk, severely restricting our capacity to dynamically assess or model the downstream effects of operational deployments on our garrison healthcare mission. The goal of this initiative is to acquire a comprehensive solution that natively links these critical domains, providing a single, integrated view to meet the dynamic demands of both garrison healthcare and the operational environment.
